name: Mid-level standard brief — team members data table, tests component accuracy

input.userMessage: Design a Team Members page. Searchable table with columns: Name (avatar + name), Email, Role (Admin/Editor/Viewer), Status (Active/Invited/Deactivated), Last Active. Invite button in header. Each row has 3-dot menu: Change Role, Deactivate, Remove. Search bar + role filter dropdown at top.

expectedBehaviors: Matches data-table layout pattern from layout-patterns.json
Uses Table component from Untitled UI PRO library (not card grid)
Plans page header with 'Invite member' button
Plans search bar + role filter dropdown at top
Plans 3-dot menu per row with Change Role, Deactivate, Remove actions
Status badges differentiate Active (green), Invited (yellow), Deactivated (gray)
Component coverage >= 75% (Table, Button, Input, Badge, Avatar, Dropdown)
Generates states: empty (no members), loading, error, success (member invited toast)
Stress-test: long emails, 1000+ members, all same role
Text uses product terminology from product.json
antiPatterns: Missing the 3-dot row menu (explicit in the brief)
Using a card grid instead of a table (brief says 'table')
Ignoring the invite flow success state
Using generic text instead of product terminology

evaluationCriteria.briefFidelity: Does every element from the brief appear in the output?
evaluationCriteria.tableNotCards: Is the primary data display a table, not a card grid?
evaluationCriteria.rowActions: Does each row have the 3-dot menu with the specified actions?
evaluationCriteria.edgeCaseCoverage: Are empty table, invite success, and deactivation states planned?
